TextENGNew: ``No, no, please don't worry about the hour.@k@vS09/11/170700091. I may not be getting overtime, but I am very much on duty.``
I opened with a casual greeting, trying to gauge Ooishi's stance.
The odds of Ooishi being one of their pawns were genuinely fifty-fifty.
If he was their agent, he would try to maneuver me—either luring me into an arrest or fishing for the scrapbooks' location.
...But determining that wouldn't be straightforward.
I couldn't easily tell whether Ooishi was friend or foe, and regardless, I had no choice but to tell him the scrapbooks existed.
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: My heart hammered wildly.
Even if Ooishi turned out not to be their pawn... I would still have to make him believe that outlandish story.@k Over the phone, no less.
Worrying about it wouldn't help.
I'd known from the start this was a gamble with little chance of winning.@k If I didn't place my bet, the wheel wouldn't even spin!
``I mentioned this briefly before, but... I really do seem to be targeted by someone.``
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``Are you certain?``
``Yes.@k@vS09/02/170201011. Before, I thought it might just be my imagination, but since yesterday, I'm convinced beyond any doubt.``
``...Hmm, 'convinced beyond doubt,' you say?``
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``Several cars seem to be watching my movements.@k@vS09/02/170201013. One of them is a white van carrying four men disguised as gardeners—likely a strike team.``
``...A strike team, you say—as in the Sonozaki family's assassination squad?``
``Yes.@k@vS09/02/170201015. I'm almost certain. They came all the way to my school today.@k@vS09/02/170201016. I barely managed to escape, but I'm in real danger.``
``...But why so suddenly?@k@vS09/11/170700096. ...Could they have gotten it into their heads that you've been poking around the Tomitake murder?``
...Ooishi didn't mention the scrapbooks.
The fact that he used a somewhat distant reference—'the Tomitake murder'—felt slightly unnatural.
But since I hadn't told Ooishi about the scrapbooks, it could well be a perfectly legitimate response.

``Actually, I just heard something troubling.@k@vS09/11/170700098. ...I would have liked to verify it more before bringing it to you, but we did promise to share new information with each other.@k@vS09/11/170700099. So let me tell you now.``
``...It seems the Sonozaki-gumi in Okinomiya are making some disturbing moves.``
``...Disturbing moves from the Sonozaki-gumi...``
``Yes.@k@vS09/11/170700102. ...This may come as quite a shock.``
``It appears they've ordered their men to track down a certain individual.``
``Track down...``
My heart lurched once.@k ...Could it be...?
``...I could scarcely believe it myself.@k@vS09/11/170700105. But the person they're searching for... is apparently you.@k@vS09/11/170700106. They're looking for Rena Ryuugu.``
``This information just came in, so I don't know their motive yet, but there's no question your name is being mentioned.``
``Do you believe me now? That I feel my life is in danger?``
``...Yes.@k@vS09/11/170700109. Our stories match perfectly.@k@vS09/11/170700110. ...Ryuugu-san, what is going on?@k@vS09/11/170700111. Why would the Sonozaki-gumi be after you?``
What they were really after were the scrapbooks I possessed.
They surely wanted to know where the scrapbooks were hidden, but they must have assumed I was keeping them concealed.
...Which meant they believed that if they could silence me, the scrapbooks would be buried forever.
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: But rather than lead with that, I deliberately turned the question back to Ooishi.
``Do you have any ideas on your end, Ooishi-san?@k@vS09/02/170201021. I... truly have no idea why...``

``Neither do I.@k@vS09/11/170700113. ...I can't fathom why the Sonozaki-gumi would mobilize against a single individual like you.@k@vS09/11/170700114. But it's happening.@k@vS09/11/170700115. ...There must be a reason.``
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``What if it's not the Sonozaki-gumi that's interested in me... but the Sonozaki main house itself?``

``But that's almost always internal business within their circles. There's never been a case where they targeted an ordinary civilian.``
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``...Now, now, Ryuugu-san.@k@vS09/11/170700121. Calm down, calm down.@k@vS09/11/170700122. Isn't it your turn to share what you know?``

``Ryuugu-san.@k@vS09/11/170700124. The Sonozaki-gumi don't mobilize like this at the drop of a hat.@k@vS09/11/170700125. Even a misunderstanding wouldn't trigger this easily.@k@vS09/11/170700126. There must be a reason. One you know.``

changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: Friend or foe... even now, at this critical juncture, I couldn't tell.@k Damn it all—make your decision, Rena Ryuugu...!
``I think it's probably... because I'm in possession of Miyo-san's scrapbooks.``
``Miyo-san?@k@vS09/11/170700133. Miyo Takano?@k@vS09/11/170700134. Her... scrapbooks...@k@vS09/11/170700135. What on earth are those?``
If Ooishi were the enemy, he wouldn't be able to suppress his excitement here.
But his tone remained level, and there was no suspicious inflection when he said 'scrapbooks.'

``Yes.@k@vS09/02/170201028. ...The truth is, a few days before Miyo-san was killed, I ran into her at the library,@k@vS09/02/170201029. and we hit it off. She lent me several scrapbooks containing her research.``
``Her research, you say?@k@vS09/11/170700137. Hmm, hmm.``
``Yes.@k@vS09/02/170201031. Miyo-san had been conducting extensive research into the history of Hinamizawa.@k@vS09/02/170201032. She'd compiled it all into several scrapbooks.``
``...When they killed Miyo-san, I believe they discovered that several scrapbooks were missing, and realized she'd entrusted them to me.``
``They're after the scrapbooks?@k@vS09/11/170700139. ...Ryuugu-san, what in the world is in them?!``
``They're a compilation of her research into Hinamizawa's ancient past, when it was still called Onigafuchi.@k@vS09/02/170201035. They document the origins of the Oyashiro-sama faith.``
``The Oyashiro-sama faith... you mean the one about@k@vS09/11/170700141. man-eating demons' descendants becoming transcendents and all that?``

``Yes.@k@vS09/02/170201037. In the course of her research, Miyo-san uncovered a critical secret about the Oyashiro-sama faith.@k@vS09/02/170201038. Her findings were so devastating that the powers behind the faith couldn't allow them to exist.``
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ``...I see, I see... The most fanatical believers couldn't tolerate the existence of that research...``
It sounded like Ooishi was taking notes on the other end of the line.@k Beneath his words, I could hear the scratch of a pencil moving rapidly.
changeReason: Translation differs significantly in the amount of detail conveyed.

TextENGNew: ``They killed Miyo-san, and now they're trying to destroy the scrapbooks she left behind.``
``At first they weren't sure whether I had them or not, so they watched me... but since yesterday, it feels like they've switched to a policy of 'eliminate anyone suspicious.'``
``...I see...@k@vS09/11/170700144. ...That's not a good situation at all.@k@vS09/11/170700145. Let me arrange protection for you.@k@vS09/11/170700146. I'll send people right away.``
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``Ah... no, I mean... there's a high probability that their agents have infiltrated the police.@k@vS09/02/170201042. So I don't need police protection.``
``I'll protect myself.@k@vS09/02/170201044. The same goes for the scrapbooks.@k@vS09/02/170201045. They'll try to destroy them.@k@vS09/02/170201046. So I absolutely cannot hand them over to anyone.``
``...Given that the Sonozaki-gumi has mobilized against you alone... if this order truly came from the top of the Sonozaki main house, then yes, I can't deny that possibility.``
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``If the Sonozaki main house is the enemy, then as much as it pains me to admit, I can't guarantee there isn't an informant among us.``
``Exactly.@k@vS09/02/170201048. So please don't worry about me.@k@vS09/02/170201049. What I need is for you to do something else.@k@vS09/02/170201050. I have a request.``
``Go ahead.@k@vS09/11/170700151. What is it?``
``...According to Miyo-san's research,@k@vS09/02/170201052. there is a terrifying conspiracy lurking in Hinamizawa's shadows right now, and it appears to be nearing execution.``
``A conspiracy.@k@vS09/11/170700153. ...What kind of conspiracy?``
...Tension flooded through me all at once.
...If I told him everything, it would sound too much like a lie.
I should leave out the most outlandish parts.@k That would actually make the rest more believable.
``The people behind the conspiracy are fanatical worshippers of Oyashiro-sama.``
``Since the Meiji era, the Oyashiro-sama faith and its authority have steadily declined. They're trying to restore the strict, absolute faith of ancient Onigafuchi.``
``...Hmm... hmm... please continue.@k@vS09/11/170700155. I'm taking notes.``
``According to Miyo-san's research, at the very foundation of the Oyashiro-sama legend lies an ancient endemic disease.``
``The terror of that disease came to be feared as 'the curse of Oyashiro-sama,' and the person capable of treating it was revered as Oyashiro-sama and ruled the village.``
``The endemic disease was caused by a parasite unique to this region.@k@vS09/02/170201058. But over many generations, it gradually became harmless, and as you know, it causes no problems today.``
``For them, a harmless disease means no curse, and without the curse, there's no need for salvation.@k@vS09/02/170201060. And without that, Oyashiro-sama's sanctity cannot be upheld.``
``...And if they wanted to restore that sanctity,@k@vS09/11/170700159. ...would that mean... reviving the endemic disease...?``

``Exactly!@k@vS09/02/170201062. They want to reenact the legend of Oyashiro-sama from the very beginning and stage the 'Second Coming of Oyashiro-sama'!@k@vS09/02/170201063. The serial mysterious death cases were test runs for this research!``
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``The true purpose of the serial mysterious deaths was to test their research results.@k@vS09/02/170201065. And they successfully recreated the 'maggot infestation disease' from the ancient texts on Tomitake-san.@k@vS09/02/170201066. Over five years, they revived the parasite to its primordial power!``
``Their ultimate goal is to unleash the parasite, save only those who submit to Oyashiro-sama with a special cure, and create a paradise of believers alone...!``
``You're certain this is all true?!``

``The scrapbooks document the fact that the parasite has infected virtually every resident of Hinamizawa.@k@vS09/02/170201069. It's backed by medical evidence—conclusive proof of the parasitic infection.``
``...Unbelievable.@k@vS09/11/170700163. ...Damn it... that old hag Oryou, she'll have death knocking any day now anyway...@k@vS09/11/170700164. She just can't cross the River Styx alone, can she...``

``The original parasite could only survive in the Hinamizawa area,@k@vS09/02/170201071. ...but given their ideological tendencies, there's a strong possibility they've been modifying it to expand its environmental range.``
``It won't stop at Hinamizawa.@k@vS09/02/170201073. ...It could swallow Okinomiya,@k@vS09/02/170201074. or spread across the entire city,@k@vS09/02/170201075. ...or even the whole prefecture.``
``...Damn it all...@k@vS09/11/170700168. What the hell are we supposed to do?!@k@vS09/11/170700169. Who's behind this?!``

``The Three Great Families of Hinamizawa.@k@vS09/02/170201077. Most likely the head of the Sonozaki family.@k@vS09/02/170201078. Though I can't say whether Oryou is the mastermind or merely another piece on the board.@k@vS09/02/170201079. ...All I can do is call them 'them.'@k@vS09/02/170201080. I have no idea of their true scale.``

``But that doesn't add up.@k@vS09/11/170700205. If that's true, she would have already been dead the night before the festival.``
``Already been dead...?``
This was confusing.
If Miyo-san's body was found on the night of Watanagashi, and she'd been dead for over twenty-four hours... that would mean...

Compared to this terror, even the thought that the Sonozaki family was hunting me and planned to burn me alive in a drum barrel seemed trivial.
...That's how dreadful the thing climbing up inside me was.@k ...Creeping, crawling, inch by inch...

Standing in this brightly lit phone booth amid total darkness was no different from announcing 'Here I am.'
Ooishi had wanted to talk more. He clicked his tongue softly and set down the receiver.
Just then, his junior detective Kumagai came bursting into the squad room.
``Ooishi-san.@k@vS09/19/173100025. This is looking really bad!@k@vS09/19/173100026. The one who ordered the search for Rena Ryuugu is Sonozaki-gumi's Tatsuyoshi Kasai!``
``Kasai?!@k@vS09/11/170700219_02. Akane Sonozaki's right-hand man...!``
Tatsuyoshi Kasai held an extremely high rank among the boss's sworn brothers.
He had once led his own subordinate faction, but a serious injury sustained during a turf war prompted his retirement from that role. Now he served as a close aide to the boss.
He was said to be especially trusted by Akane Sonozaki, and as her personal enforcer among the militant faction, he remained a feared figure.

TextENGNew: ``What?@k@vS09/06/170600057. Rena-san isn't at home?@k@vS09/06/170600058. At this hour?``
``That's right.@k@vS09/15/1715000029. I waited in front of her house for some time, but there was no sign of her returning, so I pulled back.``
``Maybe she's at a friend's house?@k@vS09/06/170600060. For all we know, she could be at Sis's place right now, playing games with the others.``
Shion lay sprawled on the sofa, picturing Mion and the others having a rowdy time, and smiled.
``...I considered that, so I stopped by the main house as well, but she wasn't there.@k@vS09/15/1715000031. Mion-san was worried too—she said Rena-san hadn't come home.``
``...Really?@k@vS09/06/170600062. ...I wonder what happened.@k@vS09/06/170600063. Hmm.``
``It's unlikely, but she may have run into trouble with some unsavory characters.@k@vS09/15/1715000033. As a precaution, I've had people asking around whether anyone's seen her.``
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ``I think Rena-san of all people would be fine, though.@k@vS09/06/170600065. She's tougher than she looks.@k@vS09/06/170600066. Ahaha.``
``By the way... what did she want with me in the first place...?``
Kasai asked while removing his jacket.
Shion had simply told him that Rena apparently wanted to see him about something, and asked him to go meet her in person.
``Hm?@k@vS09/06/170600068. Didn't I tell you?@k@vS09/06/170600069. She said she found something you dropped.@k@vS09/06/170600070. You and your butter fingers! I told her just to throw it away.``
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``Please don't...@k@vS09/15/1715000036. In any case, I hope she's safe.``
Just then, the phone rang.
Kasai answered it while loosening his tie.
Kasai's voice was so grave that Shion instantly guessed who was on the other end.
``Who was that?@k@vS09/06/170600073. Father?``
``...Yes.@k@vS09/15/1715000042. An order from the Sonozaki main house: locate Rena-san immediately and escort her to the family estate.``
``Granny's decree?!``

``...I wonder what Rena-san's gotten herself into.@k@vS09/06/170600076. ...Ohh, do you think she'll have to get her fingernails pulled?@k@vS09/06/170600077. Poor thing.@k@vS09/06/170600078. Or is it just going to be the back of the hand?``
``Rena still hasn't come home?!``
``No.@k@vS09/03/170300437. Actually, from what I've heard, Rena left home and is hiding somewhere.``
``Hiding?!@k@vS09/01/170100786. ...Wh-What do you mean?!``
``Also—and this just came in—the police are apparently searching for Rena.``
``Wh-What...?``

``Apparently, there's already an unmarked patrol car stationed in front of Rena's house.@k@vS09/03/170300440. And it sounds like several patrol cars have already entered the village, running without sirens.``
``The police also called old man Kimiyoshi's house, asking him to use the town council's phone tree to urgently confirm Rena Ryuugu's whereabouts.``
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``...Then does that mean... the bodies we hid were found?!``
We'd buried them deep in the mountains, in the most remote, convoluted spot imaginable.@k There's no way they could have been found that easily!
``No, that's one hundred percent impossible.@k@vS09/03/170300443. I guarantee it.``
``How can you be so sure about that?``
``Well...@k@vS09/03/170300445. the truth is, those bodies... I had the Sonozaki family move them to a secure location.``
``A secure location?!``

``Now, now, now!@k@vS09/03/170300447. Don't think too hard about it!@k@vS09/03/170300448. See, that forest where we buried them—when I looked into it later, I found out there was a major development project planned for this summer.``
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``So I pulled some strings and had it taken care of.``
``I see...@k@vS09/01/170100792. You're incredibly reliable when you're on my side, but you'd be terrifying as an enemy...!``
``Heh heh heh!@k@vS09/03/170300451. Who do you think this old man is?``
``...So it's not the bodies.@k@vS09/01/170100794. Then why are the police after Rena?!``

``I don't know.@k@vS09/03/170300453. But apparently, in certain circles, word had gotten around that Teppei Houjou and Rina Mamiya were trying to exploit the Ryuugu family.@k@vS09/03/170300454. Then those two went missing, and suspicion fell on Rena.``
``I don't think she's considered a suspect just yet...@k@vS09/03/170300456. but they probably want her as a key witness or person of interest.``
``It amounts to the same thing!@k@vS09/01/170100796. Damn it!@k@vS09/01/170100797. Rena had finally gotten her peaceful life back!@k@vS09/01/170100798. We have to protect her!!``

``Obviously!@k@vS09/03/170300458. I've already contacted the Okinomiya side—told them to secure Rena before the police can get to her.@k@vS09/03/170300459. If she's brought to the main house, I can protect her completely.``
``Just hiding her isn't going to solve anything, is it...?@k@vS09/01/170100801. We need to clear her name somehow!``

``We'll figure that out after she's safe.@k@vS09/03/170300461. We can produce as many alibi witnesses as we need, and the main house has the best lawyers on retainer!@k@vS09/03/170300462. There's nothing to worry about!``
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``Yeah, that's right!@k@vS09/01/170100803. We'll protect Rena no matter what!!@k@vS09/01/170100804. Damn, all I can do is rely on you. Makes me feel pathetic!``

``Now, now—set a thief to catch a thief, as they say!@k@vS09/03/170300464. I'll pull out all the stops, so leave this to me!@k@vS09/03/170300465. I'll also need the whole village to help throw the police off the scent.``
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ``We'll spread the word that Rena was spotted near Gogura.@k@vS09/03/170300467. The wider the search area, the thinner their coverage gets.``
``R-Right! That's brilliant!!``

``This is child's play compared to what we pulled off during the dam war.@k@vS09/03/170300469. I swear I'll shield Rena to the end!@k@vS09/03/170300470. Oh, and Kei-chan.@k@vS09/03/170300471. Rena trusts you the most right now.``
``...So there's a chance she'll come to you on her own.@k@vS09/03/170300473. If she does, please take her in and keep her safe!@k@vS09/03/170300474. And let me know right away!``
``Got it!!``
``Rena's a strong girl!@k@vS09/03/170300476. ...She'll overcome this. I know it.``
``...Yeah. I think so too.``
I hesitated for just a moment.
Because... the usual Rena could overcome any hardship, but the current Rena didn't quite feel like herself.
changeReason: Both translations convey that normal Rena could overcome hardship but current Rena seems off. Phrasing differences are minor.

TextENGNew: She'd been completely taken in by Takano-san's delusional scrapbooks.
...She believed the Sonozaki family was the mastermind behind everything.@k ...Would she trust the Sonozaki family enough to put herself in their care...?
``Don't worry. Rena and I are friends too!@k@vS09/03/170300478. Once we sit down and talk honestly, she'll see it was all a misunderstanding.``
``You cleared up your misunderstanding when you talked to me, right, Kei-chan?@k@vS09/03/170300480. Heh heh heh!@k@vS09/03/170300481. I still can't believe you fell for such a wild story.``
``Yeah, well...@k@vS09/01/170100809. I can hardly believe it myself.@k@vS09/01/170100810. Probably the most embarrassing moment of Keiichi Maebara's life, good grief...``
But until I'd spoken with Mion, I'd believed every word.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ...Even after swearing the bond of friendship, I'd agonized over whether Mion might be the enemy.
...Takano-san possessed an almost bewitching allure, and I felt that same spell lived on in the writings she'd left behind.
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: Her scrapbooks had the power to lead people astray.
...They were forbidden scrapbooks—never meant to be read.
Right now, Rena was probably still ensnared by the lingering sorcery Takano-san had left behind even in death.
...If we didn't snap her out of it soon... I had the terrible feeling something truly awful was going to happen...!
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``Oh, sorry—someone's at the door.@k@vS09/03/170300483. ...Looks like the police.@k@vS09/03/170300484. I'll have to hang up now.``
``Sure, got it!``
``As of tonight, the story is that Rena's been spotted hiding near Gogura.@k@vS09/03/170300486. If the police ask you anything, just say you don't know, you haven't seen her!``
``Roger that!!``
Mion confirmed my answer and hung up.
After the line went dead... the feeling slowly crept over me that Rena was being swallowed by some tremendous, unthinkable fate.
It wasn't just the police I was worried about... What truly frightened me was that Rena, under the spell of Takano-san's writings, might do something terrible.
...That's right.
Rena... could she be at that hideout in the garbage dump?
She'd told us she used to spend time there when Rina-san was at the house and she didn't want to go home.
That's right—I seemed to recall her saying she'd hidden the scrapbooks at the hideout, too!
Right, if Rena was hiding somewhere, she must be hungry by now.@k ...I should bring her some snacks at least.
I rummaged through the snack shelf in the kitchen while Mom stared at me wide-eyed.
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``Sorry, Mom!@k@vS09/01/170100814. I need to go somewhere real quick!!``
``At this hour...?@k@vS09/00/172700009. It's too late—don't go...``
``This is important!@k@vS09/01/170100816. I'll be right back.@k@vS09/01/170100817. Oh, and... if Rena happens to come by while I'm out, just let her into my room without saying anything.``
``And if someone calls asking where Rena is, just say you don't know and hang up!``
Mom looked puzzled, but I bolted out the door before she could argue.
changeReason: Translation differs significantly in the amount of detail conveyed.

TextENGNew: I grabbed the flashlight hanging on the garage wall, slung it around my neck, straddled my bike, and launched into the darkness.
The moon was unsettlingly beautiful tonight—vivid and clear.
Something... something terrible was about to happen.
I didn't know what it was, but... it was closing in. Right there.@k Something was coming!
The moon alone seemed to know what it was, peering down at us as we flailed about in ignorance, laughing.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: Damn it!@k Go ahead and laugh if you think it's so funny!@k Laugh at this ridiculous story all you want!@k I won't be laughing!@k I'll defy it! I'll fight it!@k Whatever fate awaits us—I'll fight it with everything I have!
Rena... you'd better be there...!!
My bike needs oil, maybe?@k The damn thing was squeaking like crazy.
Great—even my bicycle is mocking us...!
